fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 32-year-old Asian American male with schizophrenia with paranoia increase.
fields.Subjective: Patient appears guarded and hypervigilant. Brother reports he's been taking haloperidol only when 'voices get really bad,' approximately twice weekly for past 6 weeks, self-adjusting dose due to feeling 'like a robot' on daily medication. Since irregular dosing, paranoid delusions intensified - now believes government agents following him and apartment bugged with listening devices. Hearing multiple voices daily warning about surveillance. Started unplugging electronics and covering windows. Sleep 3-4 hours. Appetite poor, weight down 12 lbs. Lost software job 3 weeks ago after conflicts with supervisor he believed was 'part of it.' Lives alone, brother visiting daily with increasing concern. Isolating completely. Mood 'can't trust anyone' rated 3/10. Denies substance use. Denies SI but admits thoughts of 'disappearing to get away from them,' denies HI. (Evaluated by Dr. Helen Wong, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Elaborate paranoid delusions about government surveillance, frequent auditory hallucinations

Behavioral_Health_Checkup.C_SSRS: Vague ideation about escaping situation, denies suicidal intent

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizophrenia with severe paranoid exacerbation due to self-adjusted irregular medication use.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives alone; recently unemployed; severe paranoia affecting function; brother providing daily support.
Assessment_and_Plan: Severe paranoid decompensation after taking medication only twice weekly.
Discussed need for consistent daily medication.
Will switch to second-generation antipsychotic to reduce subjective sense of 'robotness.'
Brother to supervise daily medication administration.
May need LAI if oral adherence remains poor.
Close monitoring given unemployment and isolation.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Helen Wong, MD, Psychiatrist — Asian Pacific Mental Health Center, 3700 Cultural Plaza, Los Angeles, CA 90012, Phone: (555) 883-9921, Fax: (555) 883-9922
